hypothesis: Loss aversion framing in the headline + confrontational anti-Honey badge will outperform current benefit-driven headline, because fear of losing money is 2x more motivating than gaining money (Kahneman), and the Honey privacy scandal is fresh in shoppers' minds.

changes.headline: Stop Losing Money Every Time You Shop Online
changes.hero_badge: ⚠️ PayPal's Honey sells your data. CouponSnap never will.

rationale.headline: Loss aversion framing — 'stop losing' triggers stronger emotion than 'save money'. Pain-point angle tests whether negative framing outperforms current benefit-driven copy.
rationale.hero_badge: Confrontational Honey callout. More aggressive than current gentle privacy message. Tests whether naming the competitor directly increases urgency to switch.
